datasetSource: generated_signal_pairs (TypeScript fallback equivalents) / Ice1 frozen CSV (Python implementation)
selectionUnit: all rows unless the specific variant is a one-per-event variant

evidence.note: SCORE_GE_72_AVOID_6_24H variant: not avoid_6_24(r), where avoid_6_24 = 6 <= r.hours < 24 (see lines 272-273).
SCORE_GE_72_AVOID_3_12H_LEGACY variant: not avoid_3_12(r), where avoid_3_12 = 3 <= r.hours < 12 (see lines 276-277).
TypeScript live-DB fallback equivalent for SCORE_GE_72_AVOID_6_24H.
TypeScript live-DB fallback equivalent labeled COVERAGE_75_SCORE_GE_72 for COVERAGE_GE_75_SCORE_GE_72.
Flat $10 stake used in metrics()/pnl10 computation, applied uniformly across policy variants, not policy-specific.

readWriteSafety.notes: Python script documents 'mode: local analysis only; no DB, no curl' and writes only local .md/.csv report artifacts. TypeScript fallback equivalents read live DB via Supabase RPC and only write report artifacts.

knownRisks: None of the base SCORE_GE_72* predicates themselves perform one-per-event/one-per-match deduplication; only the separately named ONE_PER_EVENT_* variants (covered by ALT1's declaration) apply grouping. Treating this family as automatically one-match-safe would be incorrect.

The Python and TypeScript implementations of the same nominal variant (e.g. SCORE_GE_72_AVOID_6_24H) are separate, independently maintained code paths reading different data sources (frozen CSV vs live DB); they have not been cross-validated to produce identical row sets on the same underlying data.
